content:
General
* The task seems to rely on the player knowing the relevant maths formula(s). Please include them in the statement, as the contribution guidelines specify that clash statements should be self-sufficient and players shouldn't be expected to have to look for a formula online (if they don't know it).

* That also implies that Reverse mode isn't suitable for the clash unless you provide a formula in the default code, but that might defeat the purpose of the mode.

* The task also lacks any key programming elements such as lists, sets, dictionaries, string manipulation, loops, conditions… Please include one or more programming elements so that the task is a programming one rather than a purely maths one.

Formatting / presentation
* Please study the formatting syntax on this page: https://www.codingame.com/playgrounds/40701/help-center/statement , and format/present your statement properly as described in the points below.

Goal
* Please format all the [[variables]].

Input
* Please include line numbers, variable names and variable types in the descriptions, and format them appropriately. Use the same variable names as the ones in the stub generator.

Constraints
* Pending finalisation. Remember to use variable names defined in other sections where relevant.

* State upper bounds whenever possible.

Cases
* Please use a greater variety of input and output values.

Thanks.

Things I could do:
* Make the measurments cubits and digits, which would also allow me to make the measurments closer to the historical values without fudging.
* Differentiate the blocks with multiple block sizes per pyramid
* Give values for the internal volume of the pyramid that have to be added to the calculation
* The pyramids are all the ones from Giza and another one (I kind fudges the block sizes), but there are some smaller pyramids, and the bass pro shop and the Cestius Pyramid in Italy.
Thanks for sharing the ideas. They could work, as long as the modified task isn't purely maths and doesn't become overly complicated. I'd be happy to take a look once there's a revised version.
